---
title: "Veteran Lung Cancer Survival Analysis"
format: html
---

This project analyses survival outcomes in the Veterans Administration lung
cancer trial, a classic dataset for time-to-event modelling in R. It compares
standard and experimental chemotherapy in patients with advanced, inoperable
lung cancer using Kaplan-Meier curves, Cox proportional hazards models,
diagnostic checks, spline terms, and an Aalen additive model.

The project is organised into three parts:

---

## Overview

A concise summary of the analysis, key survival estimates, and main model
findings. This is the quickest way to see the headline results.

↗ [Open Overview »](notebooks/veteran-lung-cancer-overview.html)

---

## Full Report

A full step-by-step analysis covering exploratory survival analysis, model
building, functional form checks, spline terms, diagnostics, and final model
interpretation.

↗ [Open Full Report »](notebooks/veteran-lung-cancer-coxph-report.html)

---

## Guide

A guided version of the analysis with collapsible sections explaining each
step, the modelling choices, and the diagnostics.

↗ [Open Guide »](notebooks/veteran-lung-cancer-coxph.html)
